rulings.Case Title: AIRPORT BOULEVARD REALTY LLC VS. HB&C ASSOCIATES LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order To Charge Interest
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER AIRPORT BOULEVARD REALTY LLC'S Motion For Order To Charge Interest. Granted as to Petitioner's motion to charge Respondent HBC Associates, LLC's membership interest in Petitioner Airport Boulevard Realty, LLC. (CCP § 708.310.) Denied, without prejudice, as to Petitioner's motion to foreclose on Respondent's membership interest in Petitioner. (Corp. Code § 17705.03(b)(3).) Respondent's request for a stay is denied. (CCP §§ 918, 918.5.) Respondent's request for appraisal is not supported by citation to any legal authority and is denied. rulings.Case Title: JASON AND JESSICA MOMENT AS TRUSTEES OF THE VS. VINCENT CONSTRUCTION GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT HOHBACH-LEWIN, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION. Overruled. (CCP § 430.10(e).) The indemnity provision is not clearly barred by Civ. Code § 2782(a). In the Second Amended Complaint in Intervention ("SACI"), Intervenors allege that the subject contract entered into between 3130 Pacific LLC and Hohbach-Lewin, Inc. incorporated the indemnification provision of Hohbach-Lewin's General Conditions. (SACI, 26.) "In cases of uncertainty not removed by the preceding rules, the language of a contract should be interpreted most strongly against the party who caused the uncertainty to exist." (Civ. Code, § 1654.) Here, the indemnity provision does not expressly contemplate the indemnitee's sole negligence, and in the demurrer, the clause is interpreted against Hohbach-Lewin, Inc. who caused the uncertainty to exist. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC., SCOTT MUNRO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to causes of action 1, 2, and 6 are sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff fails to allege exhaustion of administrative remedies. The demurrer to cause of action 5 is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not clearly allege an employment relationship. The demurrer to cause of action 17 for work related stress is sustained without leave to amend because it is uncertain and plaintiff fails to demonstrate that it is a recognized cause of action. The demurrer to cause of action 7 for fraud is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not allege the claim with the requisite particularity. The demurrer to cause of action 3 for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not clearly allege extreme and outrageous conduct. The demurrer to cause of action 4 for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not clearly allege an underlying contract between the parties. The demurrer to causes of action 8, 10, and 11 for unfair competition is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not clearly allege the unlawful, fraudulent, or unfair conduct. The demurrer to causes of action 9 and 12 is sustained without leave to amend because defendant is not a stranger to the parties' relationship. The demurrer to cause of action 13 for accounting is sustained without leave to amend because an accounting is a remedy. The demurrer to cause of action 14 for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not describe an actual controversy. The demurrer to causes of action 15 for injunction and 16 for writ of mandate is sustained without leave to amend because they are unintelligible and appear to relate to a non-party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NATHANAEL WONDERGEM VS. THE IGNATIAN CORPORATION D/B/A SAINT IGNATIUS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT THE IGNATIAN CORPORATION D/B/A SAINT IGNATIUS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to the first cause of action. Plaintiff met its burden by showing there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff engaged in protected activity by refusing to comply with practices that would violate a law or regulation. (SSF 31-33.) Plaintiff adequately pled that he refused to follow unlawful directions (First Amended Complaint, par. 33), and his declaration does not directly contradict his previous discovery responses. The motion is denied as to the second cause of action. Defendant did not meet its initial burden. Defendant introduces evidence showing Plaintiff does not know the reason for his termination; however, direct evidence of the nexus between the protected activity and the termination is not required. This nexus can be established by circumstantial evidence. (Mamou v. Trendwest Resorts, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 686, 715.) The motion is denied as to the third and fourth causes of action. Plaintiff has established the existence of a triable issue of material fact by introducing evidence showing that the Human Resources Director did not investigate Jorge Barron's accusations, and had an investigation taken place, it would have revealed that the statements were false. (SSF 103, 105-110.) This evidence, coupled with the circumstantial evidence that Plaintiff was terminated for whistleblowing, gives rise to a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant acted with malice in publishing the statement. The motion is granted as to the fifth cause of action. Plaintiff has not met his burden of establishing a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant engaged in outrageous conduct. The conduct complained of, such as disciplinary write-ups, accusations of arriving late, and not having workplace concerns adequately addressed is personnel management activity. (Janken v. GM Hughes Electronics (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 55, 80, "[a] simple pleading of personnel management activity is insufficient to support a claim of intentional infliction of emotional distress, even if improper motivation is alleged.") Teasing and name calling by co-workers also does not give rise to an intentional infliction of emotional distress claim. (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1051, "Liability for intentional infliction of emotional distress does not extend to mere insults, indignities, threats, annoyances, petty oppressions or other trivialities.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RYAN MAK et al VS. M.S.W. ENTERPRISES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFFS RYAN MAK, NORRIS MAK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted. Plaintiffs have met their prima facie burden by setting forth evidence establishing the following elements: (1) Plaintiffs and Defendant entered into contracts by executing the nine promissory notes (Ryan Mak Decl. 2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22, 22, 26, 30, Exs. A, C-I; Norris Mak Decl., 2, Ex. A); (2) Plaintiffs performed by loaning the money (Ryan Mak Decl., 3, 7, 11, 15, 19, 23, 27, 31; Norris Mak Decl. 3); (3) Plaintiffs demanded payment (Ryan Mak Decl., 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, Ex. B; Norris Mak Decl. 4, Ex. B); and (4) Defendant breached the terms of the note by not paying on demand (Ryan Mak Decl., 5, 9, 13, 17, 21, 25, 29, 33; Norris Mak Decl., 5). Therefore, the burden shifts to Defendant to raise a triable issue of material fact. (CCP sec. 437(c)(p)(1).) Defendant has not met its burden. First, the claims as to the 2005 promissory note are not barred by the statute of limitations. (Com. Code sec. 3118(b).) Second, both declarations submitted in support of the opposition are not dated, as required by CCP sec. 2015.5. Even if the Court considers the defective declarations, the declarations do not give rise to a triable issue of material fact. "When opposition to a motion for summary judgment is based on inferences, those inferences must be reasonably deducible from the evidence, and not such as are derived from speculation, conjecture, imagination, or guesswork." (Joseph E. Di Loreto, Inc. v. O'Neill (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 149, 161.) Defendant provides no evidence to give rise to the inference that Plaintiffs fraudulently induced Defendant to execute the promissory note. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CANDIDA GARCIA VS. DANIEL BORG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Written Interrogatories, And For Monetary Sanctions, Supporting Declaration Of John F. Lee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT DANIEL BORG's Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Written Interrogatories, And For Monetary Sanctions, Supporting Declaration Of John F. Lee Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses to defendant's Special Interrogatories-13 and 6-32, is granted as unopposed. Verified responses shall be served within twenty days of the date of this Order. Sanctions in the sum of $500.00 are awarded to defendant as against plaintiff's counsel to be paid within twenty days of the date of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAY P. SIOTTO et al VS. ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Compelling Deft Rock And Rose Landscaping To Produce Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF RAY SIOTTO's Motion Compelling Defendant Rock And Rose Landscaping To Produce Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel a further response to Document Request #10 is granted as modified by the plaintiff during the parties' "meet and confer" process. The motion was timely filed within 45 days from service by defendant of supplemental responses. The documents to be produced reflecting "complaints" shall be written communications (hard copy or email) from defendants clients to defendants' owners and managers and the responses thereto. The Plaintiff shall prepare a proposed Order which tracks plaintiff's modification of category #10 as set forth in the letter dated March 20, 2014. Sanctions are awarded plaintiff as against defense counsel in the sum of $1,250.00 to be paid within 20 days of the date of the Order to be entered herein. While both sides participated in an extensive "meet and confer" process, the position taken by defendants limiting the definition of "complaints" to "formalized judicial" and "non-judicial complaints" was not reasonable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRIAN MARKHAM VS. SEGWAY OF SF BAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff did not timely comply with the claims presentation requirement of the Government Claims Act. Gov. Code sec. 911.2 requires that a claim relating to a personal injury cause of action be presented no later than six months after the accrual of the cause of action. Plaintiff's cause of action accrued on August 17, 2012. Plaintiff did not file his claim until July 12, 2013. Plaintiff did not timely apply for leave to file a late claim. Plaintiff's delayed discovery argument is rejected. The complaint pleads Plaintiff was injured on August 17, 2012. "So long as a suspicion exists, it is clear that the plaintiff must go find the facts; she cannot wait for the facts to find her." (Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1110.) Further, "aggrieved parties generally need not know the exact manner in which their injuries were effected, nor the identities of all parties who may have played a role therein" for a cause of action to accrue. (Bernson v. Browning-Ferris Indus. of Calif., Inc. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 926, 932.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR LLC, VS. JENNIFER M. PEYTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR LLC's, Motion For Order To Deem Matters Admitted Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to deem matters admitted as set forth defendant's Requests for Admissions, Set One, is granted as unopposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GANADERIA BRASIL INDUSTRIA E COMERCIO DE ACESSORIO VS. LEVI STRAUSS & CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Protective Order Requiring Pltfs To Return Privileged Documents And Prohibiting Futher Use Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANTs LEVI STRAUSS & CO., MARYANN NICHOLS' Motion For A Protective Order Requiring Plaintiff's To Return Privileged Documents And Prohibiting Futher Use Thereof Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court's grants defendant's motion for a protective order, immediate return of all attorney-client and attorney work-product privileged documents, and prohibition of use of the privileged documents in the pending litigation. There was no waiver arising from the communications between counsel from December 24, 1013 and February 7, 2014. See: State Compensation Ins. Fund vs. WPS, Inc., (1999) 82 Cal. Rptr. 2d 799. The hearing is required to determine the additional discovery warranted, if any, with respect to a determination of the plaintiff's use of the privileged documents during the period of time the documents were recognized to be privileged. =(302/JPT)
